search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae hearing loss telehealth guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae hearing loss standard Abu Dhabi
site:nice.org.uk NG98 hearing loss adults recommendations
site:entnet.org sudden hearing loss guideline update
candidate_sources_rejected: older sudden-hearing-loss guidance as complete coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
NG98 provides the broader current adult assessment and referral framework.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults over 18; children and congenital or paediatric hearing pathways are not covered.
setting_applicability: Exact for UK primary, community, audiology, and secondary care; not validated for UAE referral pathways.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE hearing-loss guideline was identified; NICE NG98 requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98, last updated and reviewed 2023-10-02 with a non-practice link update in 2025, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: NG98 remains the current NICE adult hearing-loss guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped hearing-difficulty, sudden or rapid change, wax, infection, audiology, and urgent referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped unilateral, tinnitus, vertigo, neurological, ear pain, discharge, abnormal-ear, and clinician-arranged referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped history, communication, function, otoscopy, audiology, hearing-device discussion, questions, support, and follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children, ear fullness, noise exposure specificity, recent trauma, whisper or tuning-fork tests, head trauma, and fixed two-week result-review timing remain unsupported.
Conductive or sensorineural classification remains clinician-stated and test dependent.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
